question.text: Within how many business days of the NFO closure must allotment of units be completed for non-ELSS schemes?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI mandates that allotment of units in an NFO must be completed within 5 business days from the date of NFO closure for all schemes except ELSS. This ensures investors are not kept waiting indefinitely and their funds are deployed promptly. **The Trap:** 3 business days is the timeline for redemption payouts in equity schemes, not for NFO allotment. Candidates frequently mix up these two distinct timelines. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A covers NFO processes; Series II-A (RTA Corporate) covers allotment timelines in corporate actions context. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Track the NFO closure date and add 5 business days to set client expectations for when units will reflect in their folio. Any delay beyond this is a compliance issue worth escalating.
